---
title: "Troubleshooting & Logs"
---

## Issue With Executing Commands And Streaming

Issue: While trying to execute a command or perform streaming capabilities using Hyland Desktop Host, it fails to work as expected and the following error message is found in the DesktopHostCore_Log.json file: "Message":" chunk Size violated size limit exceeded".

Solution: Desktop Host is configured to exchange data and communicate with its underlying components through messages. You can configure the default message size for Desktop Host.

To configure the message size for Desktop Host:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor with elevated privileges. In a typical installation, this file is in one of the following locations:
- After Windows installation, the file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.
- After macOS installation, the file is in /Library/Hyland/DesktopHost/bin.
2.  In the file, add the following text:
```
{
   "MaximumReceiveMessageSize": 128
}
```

Note: If the size of any message exceeds the default value of 128 KB, the connection will discontinue. Though a lower value may enhance performance, it is not recommended.
3.  Save the file and close the text editor.
4.  Restart the computer.

## Logging Out

To log out of the Hyland Capture application:

1.  Click the user menu icon in the upper-right corner of the interface to expand the user menu drop-down list.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  Select Log Out. The application is closed, and a confirmation message is displayed.

## Resetting Your Password

When you click Forgot Password in the Hyland Capture application's login dialog box, you are prompted to confirm your email address.

To reset your password:

1.  Confirm or update your Hyland Experience email address in the field provided and click Submit. An email with a link to reset your password is sent to the address you provide.
2.  In the email sent from the Hyland Experience platform, click the reset password link. The reset password page is displayed.
3.  Enter your new password in the New Password field.
4.  Enter the password again in the Confirm New Password field.
5.  Click Reset. A confirmation message is displayed. For information on logging in to the Hyland Capture application with your new password, see Opening the Hyland Capture Application.
